title: carmichaelia nana
intro: Carmichaelia nana is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae, that is native to New Zealand. It is also known as the dwarf broom or small broom. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Carmichaelia nana include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, from colds and flu to skin conditions and respiratory problems.
